message:
fix(share): probe retry refactor, LEFT JOIN for missing senders, and recipient aggregation

Probe:
- Refactored probe_get_inner() to use a new send_with_retries() helper
  that encapsulates retry logic, replacing the inline retry-on-error
  pattern. The redirect-following loop is now cleaner with retries
  handled at the single-request level rather than the redirect level.

Static render:
- Changed agents JOIN from INNER to LEFT JOIN in discover_messages()
  so messages with deleted/missing sender agents are still included
  in the rendered static site instead of being silently dropped.
- Added render_with_missing_sender_agent_preserves_message() test
  verifying messages with NULL sender_id render correctly.
- Fixed body redaction to use the configured body_placeholder from
  the redaction policy instead of always using the hardcoded
  BODY_REDACTED_PLACEHOLDER constant.
- Updated thread page rendering to show the actual (redacted) body
  text in snippets rather than the placeholder constant.
- Added redact_bodies check to per-message page rendering to ensure
  consistent redaction state across thread and message views.

Finalize (materialized views):
- Changed recipient query from single-recipient lookup (LIMIT 1) to
  GROUP_CONCAT aggregation that collects ALL recipients for a message,
  ordered alphabetically for deterministic output.
- Used LEFT JOIN for recipient-to-agent resolution so recipients with
  deleted agents still appear (as empty string) rather than being dropped.
- Added test verifying multi-recipient aggregation produces sorted,
  comma-separated output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
